From I-HY_2022_14-ACC-00: "# C5 Subcontractor Management Plan
(1) The Contractor shall within 30 days of the Contract Date submit a Subcontractor Management Plan (SMP) to the Project Manager for information and comments, if any.
(2) The Contractor shall then submit quarterly the updated SMP, until the issuance of the certificate of Completion or where there is more than one such certificate, the issuance of the last certificate of Completion, to the Project Manager for information and comments, if any. Should there be any" From DEVB TC(W) No. 3_2023: "(11) The \*Contractor Contractor shall submit monthly report on implementation of SSSS components on site to \*Project Manager Engineer / Surveyor / Supervising Officer / Service Manager Maintenance Surveyor in advance of the monthly Site Safety Management Committee (SSMC) meeting for record and discussion in the SSMC meeting. The monthly report shall include, but not be limited to, updates on site SSSS components setup, operation, malfunction, interruption and modification, etc. The report shal"
reasoning: The tender clause C5 concerns the submission, updating, and content requirements of a Subcontractor Management Plan (SMP), including timelines, change notifications, and reference to Appendix B guidelines. None of the candidate clauses address subcontractor management. Candidate 11 relates to monthly reporting on Smart Site Safety System (SSSS) components, which is safety-monitoring focused and unrelated to subcontractor oversight. Candidates 2 and 3 pertain to methods of measurement in Bills of Quantities and Smart Site Safety System components, respectively—both irrelevant to SMP requirements. Therefore, no candidate matches the subject matter of C5.

1) The Contractor shall be fully responsible for its design under the contract (including the Contractor’s Design and Cost Savings Design) as well as the general performance of the contract. reasoning: The STANDARD clause specifies technical requirements for automation, remote sensing systems, accuracy thresholds (≥90%), live trials, alert transmission to a central platform, and monthly reporting. The TENDER clause addresses Intellectual Property Rights (IPR) obligations and warranties, with no reference to automation systems, performance accuracy, alert mechanisms, or reporting requirements. There is no overlap in subject matter.

xx.1 Rates appearing in this Section of the Method of Measurement shall be deemed to allow for the value of work in conn"
reasoning: The tender clause D25 primarily addresses two distinct topics: (1) the regulated disposal of construction and demolition waste at designated or approved alternative sites, and (2) participation in a Pay for Safety Performance Merit Scheme (PFSPMS), which ties certain payments to safety performance reporting and compliance. Candidate 1 (clause_id=4) relates to payment items for a Smart Site Safety System involving Virtual Reality training and centralized digital platforms—topics not mentioned in D25. Candidate 2 (clause_id=12) concerns automated warning systems for mobile plant danger zones, which is a specific safety equipment requirement unrelated to either waste disposal or performance-based safety payments. Neither candidate addresses the core obligations in D25 regarding disposal grounds or the PFSPMS framework, making both irrelevant to the subject matter.

reasoning: The tender clause D28 'Pay for Safety Performance Merit Scheme (PFSPMS)' relates to incentivizing and monitoring contractor safety performance, which aligns closely with Candidate 11’s requirement for the Contractor to submit monthly reports on the implementation of Smart Site Safety System (SSSS) components, including incident summaries, response actions, and site monitoring data. These reporting obligations support performance-based safety schemes like PFSPMS by providing measurable, auditable safety performance data. Candidate 11 directly addresses systematic safety performance tracking and reporting, making it more relevant than Candidate 12 (focused on mobile plant proximity alerts) or Candidate 'general' (which discusses variation orders for adopting SSSS but not ongoing performance reporting). The tender clause is compliant as it is consistent with the intent and mechanism described in Candidate 11.

None of the candidate clauses address public health, vaccination, or access control based on health documentation. Candidate 1 relates to measurement methods for Smart Site Safety Systems (SSSS).
